item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pedantic as one may be, the hamster pictured looks like a dwarf - maybe a djungarian or a hybrid? Wish that could be updated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm half awake so I'm probably overcomplicating this.

the tattoo thing… is this because tattoos can be interpreted as images or because of some obscure leviticus thing Ezekiel imparted thru his reality bending?

item.title: Re: New image
item.content:encoded: Will probably find a cuter picture when I get the chance. That's a big part of the presentation.

item.title: New image
item.content:encoded: Replaced with CC compliant one with Lily's permission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was so cute tho

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Please delete the image on this page I don't have the rights.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not easy to give me Suriphobia (hamster-phobia), but you did it. Congratulations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would also like to know if 1616 ages and therefore will it expire in a few years.

From article I got impression it is totally normal hamster beside that one anomalous property. So it is as easy to kill as any other hamster and yes, problem of SCP-1616 will solve itself in few years.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That would be actually good argument for D subject to be far away from Nibbles during experiment… I guess writer went for "that cute "petting of hamster" scene would make later event more horryfying".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-1616 was created in SCP-2782. It is referenced as Wormwood in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this should be so hilarious, but its terrifying XD
Well deserved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The question is, if this hamster looked at a mirror, would it regurgitate the mirror, or would it regurgitate itself? And if it did regurgitate itself, would it start nibbling on itself? Would it lose body mass or gain body mass?

EDIT: I really have to read through everything more. Now for another question; what does this have to do with SCP-2782?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great, now my nope-o-meter's broken.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Never before have I been terrified of a hamster, and never for eye-horror reasons
plz send halp
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This disturbs me on so many levels.
But in a good way.
Upvote! :)

item.title: How have I not seen this before?
item.content:encoded: That escalated quickly. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Guys look I found Nibbles IRL

item.title: Rule #1 of the SCP Foundation
item.content:encoded: The more adorable the SCP, the more scary as balls it is (for example, this one, 1048 ahd 367.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am now scared of hamsters. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I always forget how terrifying this thing is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Show it a picture of SCP-096.

Who? Will? Win?

item.title: Re: Not a Mod Post
item.content:encoded: Eesh, and you guys tell me not to rush my SCPs…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Unless the hamster can contain an entire planet in its mouth (rather then a single eyeball) it isn't even close to being Keter."

Well, it's shown that it was able to hold an entire baby elephant in its mouth…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Keter is more reserved for "oh-my-gash-its-the-end-of-the-world" SCPs.

No, actually, it's not.

The designation "Keter" is assigned to subjects that both (a) display vigorous, active hostility to human life, civilization, and/or spacetime, and (b) are capable of causing significant destruction in the event of a containment breach. Such subjects must be cataloged, contained according to special containment procedures, and destroyed, if possible.

What you're thinking of is an "XK" tagged article. SCP-058, SCP-106, and SCP-835 are all Keter but are nothing close to world-ending.

This thing is tagged as Keter because any object it has seen before, ANY can be removed from wherever it is and pushed out through its mouth. Suppose it produces the head of a researcher, or a government leader, or the entire Taj Mahal? It also continuously destroys its cell.

item.title: Okay, Now This One Is A Mod Post
item.content:encoded:
I'm just going to go ahead and advise people to not let TDLZ's comment derail the thread on this SCP. The object class has been discussed and defended upthread, as Reject as pointed out; accept or reject his explanation as you see fit and vote accordingly. No need to get into a slap fight, which is what most of TDLZ's conversations have turned into.

TDLZ, since you're new here, let me just tell you that you are not to respond to Mod Posts, which is what this is. This line of conversation is closed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Post moved.

item.title: Not a Mod Post
item.content:encoded: I'm going to have to suggest that you, who have been here all of two days, defer to someone like Famine on the subject of what object classes are "reserved for."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Addressed upthread by the author.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sorry, but i don't see why this is Keter. Admittedly, it's definitely Euclid, because it can definitely breach containment with time and even kill humans if it had more intelligence, but it can be relatively easily recontained, and damage it causes to cell walls can be repaired. Not to mention, due to its lack of intelligence, it only uses its ability when it's hungry, so it's doubtful that it could really attack people with it.

Keter is more reserved for "oh-my-gash-its-the-end-of-the-world" SCPs. Unless the hamster can contain an entire planet in its mouth (rather then a single eyeball) it isn't even close to being Keter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not completely original, this was inspired by a Twilight Zone episode called toys of Caliban.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're commenting on page about a (fictional, but still…) hamster that nibbles on human eyes. It's rather difficult to sound like a psycho. Anyway, on to your question.
As I see it there are two possible outcomes of this:
1) It doesn't work and instead of getting something that's right before it's eyes Nibbles starts to pull objects from it's tiny hamster memory. And who knows what does it remember?
2) It does work and permanetly disables all Nibble's anomalous properties. However great love for furballs is not the reason why Foundation keeps 1616 alive and I believe that from Foundation's standpoint killing Nibbles and "rendering him harmless" would be exactly the same.

I have to say I don't like this article. While the concept itself is amusing and experiment log is well done there are some things in this article that don't sit right with me.
First of all it's mentioned two times that 1616 shouldn't be exposed to illustrations (tattoos). So how does it work? Obviously it can teleport something that it has seen into it's cheecks but that requires a teleported object to actually exist. Obviously it works with photographs (though, what about outdated photographs?) but how will it teleport something that has never been in the first place? If I show it a painting of a flower that never existed will it be able to just materialize a flower out of thin air? That gets us to the next question: can it materialize something that does not have a real prototype? Like an angel? (Nevermind Clef's proposal). How abstract a drawn object must be in order that 1616 couldn't get it? Does level of detail on an illustration matter?

I refuse to believe that Foundation never set experiments to reveal limitations to it's ablities and the article doesn't touch this theme at all. At least a couple of [DATA EXPUNGED] would be nice.

I would also like to know if 1616 ages and therefore will it expire in a few years. That's not a big deal but it's something of importance for reaserch and containment purposes. And some history on how Foundation obtained Nibbles would be good too.
Overall it's -1 from me for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't mean to sound like a psychopath but… why not just gouge out its eyes? That would render it harmless (hopefully) without killing it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ilu kens

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was going to post that if nobody else had.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Food pump directly to stomach, both eyes amputated. Kept under heavy sedation.

There, problem solved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A very nice article, and an original idea too. It'll likely stick in my mind whenever I see a hamster eat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do you mean this one?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, what Voct said. Also, excessive weird. I believe that there is a guide for that on this wiki that you can find with a simple search.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Occam's razor. Don't attribute more anomalous properties to an object or entity than are necessary to explain observed phenomena. We have no reason that he's more intelligent than other hamsters, or that he's from another timeline, or that he's a robot. Just that he does this weird space-bending thing in his mouth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But he's an anomalous hamster.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: actually, hamsters don't have mirror recognition. So he'd just cheekpouch the mirror itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been thinking about the whole "Saw himself in a mirror" thing. I'm assuming that if he did, he would just create a copy of himself in his cheek, and inside of that copy's cheek there would be another copy, and so on and so forth. So basically what you'd get is a hamster that perpetually vomits hamsters.

damnit voct

item.title: Re: Sentient?
item.content:encoded: Hope this helps.

item.title: Sentient?
item.content:encoded: Wait, why is this tagged as sentient?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not thinking big enough. How about the whole goddamn planet. Just sucked into it and spit out. Even if the population survived, after the initial shock and everything, our orbit would probably be different. And the moon.

Oh right. Probably seen the moon too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And then we end up with 42 of them running around the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Or the continent.

Some people might consider that important.

Y'know, because they were standing on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Here's something. Assuming if it sees a picture of something, it can regurgitate it. So assuming that, if we showed it a picture of itself…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If it even tried, the potential damage it could do to the site alone warrants Keter. This things cheeks would become a portal to the sun. Or worse, would expel a portion of the sun. Depending on how big a portion, it could literally destroy the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah. Sorry. I have found that the easiest way to prove a point is to put them in that scenario. I apologize for not clarifying better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, no, I was talking about Fire. Good that the matter was clarified.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I see what you mean now, I retract my earlier statement. I guess some people just respond to these situations on extreme ends of the spectrum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just to clarify. I was not insulting you. I was debating your opinion. Putting you as that Class D who found their eye taken into the mouth of a hamster without it even touching them in like 20 minutes. Still seeing through the eye, feeling the pain of it biting and nibbling on it, without it even being in your head now. Not trying to insult you in any way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think he meant, "you're kind of fucked up [after the hamster has eaten your eyes]"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Me? Oh don't get me wrong, I've seen plenty of intelligent, mature writers here. Just blatantly saying someone is fucked up because of a difference of opinion was out of line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Me, him, or both of us?

I'm confused. Head hurts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm… I'm sure, or at least I hope, he misphrased that.
